summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, developing programs grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate CEO/HR discovery and {cross|stakeholder interviews} to diagnose absenteeism drivers; analyze leave data to quantify costs
Conduct focus groups and 1:1 semi-structured interviews with an NGO leadership team to surface {cross|decision-making and accountability gaps}
Analyze qualitative {data|interview data} to identify themes, then challenge the leadership team to redesign cross-functional processes
Establish {cross|cross-functional} processes and {coord|documentation} for 70+ staff, streamlining {coord|operations} and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate listening sessions and needs assessments with 25 global {cross|stakeholders}, resulting in 3 new learning programs best aligned with needs
Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining {coord|program positioning} and {cross|communications strategy} via {data|feedback analysis}
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, boosting {prog|program engagement} and achieving a 78% {prog|program enrollment rate}
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on {data|satisfaction drivers}
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal training on DEI, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
Improved employee experience via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

jd.meta: Forth Program Associate — Portland OR hybrid, nonprofit electric mobility, $49.5–59.5K

changeLog: 2026-06-05: Tailored from base resume.json for Forth Program Associate (Portland nonprofit, electric-mobility). Recipe: Program Management/Engagement. Summary identity = Program Management professional. InGenius title = Program Manager, Enablement. InGenius bullets = Set B (cross-functional processes, listening sessions, communications strategy, 78% program enrollment rate). ODN = 3 bullets covering both projects using Pair B diagnostic framing (stakeholder interviews, qualitative data analysis, process redesign) — best match for JD's 'collect/analyze project data + stakeholder convening' duties. Header location = Portland, OR (role is Portland). Skills: removed HRIS/ATS/LMS (not JD-relevant for a nonprofit program coord role); added Google Workspace (JD-relevant for nonprofit ops); kept foundational MS Office, Data Analysis, PM tools; Notion retained as JD-gated (nonprofit knowledge mgmt); Languages line kept prominent (JD lists multilingual as a 'helpful' criterion — genuine hit). Bullet reordering: NextGen leads with 2,000+ employee experiences bullet (data collection/analysis — closest match to JD's data-collection duty).
